mahavira: Ahimsa (non-violence); anekantavada (many-sidedness of truth); liberation through asceticism
buddha: Four Noble Truths; Eightfold Path; impermanence and non-self (anatta)
nagarjuna: Emptiness (sunyata); two truths doctrine; middle way between eternalism and nihilism
shankara: Brahman alone is real; world is illusion (maya); Atman is Brahman; liberation through knowledge
ramanuja: Qualified non-dualism; souls as real parts of Brahman; bhakti (devotion) path to liberation
laozi: The Tao that can be told is not the eternal Tao; wu wei (non-action); simplicity and naturalness
confucius: Ren (benevolence); li (ritual propriety); the junzi (noble person) cultivates virtue
mozi: Universal love without partiality; consequentialist ethics; opposition to offensive warfare
mencius: Human nature is inherently good; benevolent government; four beginnings of virtue
zhuangzi: Butterfly dream (what is reality?); embrace spontaneity; all perspectives are relative
xunzi: Human nature is evil; goodness is acquired; ritual and education transform nature
han_fei: Law and punishment maintain order; the ruler must be impersonal; human nature is self-interested
thales: First philosopher; rational principles in nature
anaximander: Apeiron as first principle; rational cosmology
heraclitus: Flux over permanence; paradox over logic; logos invoked but reason never systematized — seeds of irrationalism
parmenides: Being is one; logical monism over the senses
empedocles: Four elements; natural causation without myth
democritus: Atomism; materialist reason; void and atoms
protagoras: Man is the measure; cognitive relativism
thrasymachus: Justice is the advantage of the stronger
socrates: Dialectic method; logos; the examined life
plato: Eternal Forms; objective truth; reason over senses
aristotle: Logic; empiricism; objective science and ethics
lucretius: De Rerum Natura; atomism; reason over religion
chrysippus: Systematic Stoic logic; master of the syllogism
cicero: Natural law; rational statecraft and rhetoric
epictetus: Logos; only what is in our rational control
plotinus: The One beyond reason; mystical emanation; reality is graded emanation from ineffable source
porphyry: Neoplatonist mystic; reason as ladder to supra-rational unity
augustine: Faith over reason; original sin; City of God; divine illumination
proclus: Systematic Neoplatonism; divine intellect above discursive reason
al_kindi: First Arab philosopher; reason and revelation compatible
al_ghazali: Incoherence of philosophers; faith over reason
anselm: Faith precedes reason; ontological argument subordinates logic to theology — reason as servant of revelation
maimonides: Rationalist theology; reason compatible with Torah
thomas_aquinas: Reason and faith reconciled; natural law
bernard_of_clairvaux: Love and faith over dialectical reason
meister_eckhart: Mystical union with God beyond rational concepts
erasmus: Christian humanism; rational reform of the Church
machiavelli: Political reason divorced from religious morality
giordano_bruno: Infinite universe; reason and science over dogma
la_boetie: Discourse on Voluntary Servitude; tyranny rests on consent; liberty through refusal
montaigne: Que sais-je? — radical self-doubt suspends all objective knowledge; skepticism as a way of life
francis_bacon: Inductive method; idols of the mind; new science
john_locke: Tabula rasa; experience as the ground of knowledge
george_berkeley: Esse est percipi — existence requires a perceiver; denies mind-independent reality
rene_descartes: Cogito; clear and distinct ideas; method of doubt
baruch_spinoza: Geometric ethics; reason as the highest freedom
malebranche: Vision in God; occasionalism; Cartesian theology
leibniz: Principle of sufficient reason; monads; calculus
hugo_grotius: Natural law foundation; law of nations; rights independent of theology
john_milton: Areopagitica; freedom of thought and press as absolute; individual conscience
algernon_sidney: Discourses Concerning Government; republican liberty; pre-Locke rights theory
isaac_newton: Mathematical principles of natural philosophy; universal gravitation; scientific method
blaise_pascal: Heart has reasons reason cannot know; the wager
giambattista_vico: Verum factum: we know only what we make — denies objective reality independent of human construction
montesquieu: Rational analysis of laws; separation of powers
voltaire: Reason vs. superstition, tyranny, and fanaticism
adam_smith: Rational self-interest; invisible hand; moral sentiments
lessing: Rational theology; toleration; education of humanity
condorcet: Indefinite human progress through reason and science
john_adams: Mixed government; balance of powers; republican virtue
thomas_paine: Natural rights; popular sovereignty; anti-monarchy; "Common Sense"
thomas_jefferson: Natural rights; "life, liberty, pursuit of happiness"; separation of church and state
james_madison: Federalism; checks and balances; faction theory; Bill of Rights
mary_wollstonecraft: A Vindication of the Rights of Woman; reason has no sex; equality through education
wilhelm_von_humboldt: The Limits of State Action; individual development as the highest end; minimal government
david_hume: Causation is habit not necessity; induction unjustifiable — destroys the rational foundations of science
rousseau: Nature and sentiment over civilization and reason
edmund_burke: Tradition and prejudice over abstract rationalism
hamann: Faith and language over Enlightenment reason
jacobi: Leap of faith; Glaubensphilosophie against Spinoza
herder: Cultural particularity; Volksgeist over universal reason
immanuel_kant: Thing-in-itself unknowable; reason bounded; duty-ethics divorced from reality — arch-enemy of Objectivism
jeremy_bentham: Felicific calculus: aggregate utility can override individual rights — collectivism in rational disguise
de_maistre: Theocratic authority and tradition over rationalism
fichte: Absolute Ego as primary; collapses objective reality into subject
hegel: Dialectical Spirit; individual dissolved into collective History; root of totalitarianism
schelling: Nature philosophy; subject-object identity; late mysticism
auguste_comte: Three stages; science replaces theology and metaphysics
frederic_bastiat: The Law; property rights; the state as legal plunder; broken window fallacy
alexis_de_tocqueville: Democracy in America; liberty over equality; tyranny of the majority; civic virtue
frederick_douglass: Natural rights to demolish slavery; reason as liberation; self-ownership
lord_acton: Power corrupts absolutely; liberty as the supreme political value; conscience over authority
ralph_waldo_emerson: Over-Soul; intuition over reason; nature mysticism; self-reliance
henry_david_thoreau: Civil Disobedience; mystical individualism; simplicity; conscience over law
max_stirner: The Ego and Its Own; all abstractions are spooks; pure egoism; the unique one
lysander_spooner: No Treason; natural rights; Constitution has no authority; individual over state
auberon_herbert: Voluntary taxation; no coercion ever justified; individual sovereignty; the true liberty
pierre_joseph_proudhon: Property is theft; mutualist anarchism; federalism; anti-state, anti-capitalist
mikhail_bakunin: Collectivist anarchism; destroy the state and God; revolutionary violence
friedrich_engels: Co-author of Communist Manifesto; historical materialism; dialectics of nature
peter_kropotkin: Mutual aid; communist anarchism; no individual property; scientific anarchism
schopenhauer: Blind Will underlies reality; reason is its servant
nietzsche: Will to Power; death of God; perspectivism
john_stuart_mill: Harm principle subordinates individual to society; utility trumps rights — altruism with a logical veneer
kierkegaard: Leap of faith; subjectivity is truth; against Hegel
karl_marx: Dialectical materialism inverts Hegel but retains irrationalism; collectivism; anti-individual
charles_peirce: Truth as long-run consensus of inquirers — replaces correspondence to reality with social agreement
william_james: Truth is what works — explicitly rejects correspondence theory of truth; pragmatic cash-value
john_dewey: Instrumentalism; truth as tool for biological adaptation; no objective standards independent of use
gottlob_frege: Logical foundations of mathematics; objectivity of sense
edmund_husserl: Phenomenological reduction brackets objective reality; consciousness constitutes the world
henri_bergson: Élan vital; intuition over intellect and mechanism
max_weber: Disenchantment: reason strips meaning from the world; value-free science cannot ground ethics — fact-value split
alfred_whitehead: Process philosophy; reality as becoming not being; organism over mechanism
emma_goldman: Anarcho-communism; free love; anti-capitalism; individual freedom through collective liberation
albert_jay_nock: Our Enemy the State; radical individualism; the remnant; anti-mass society
h_l_mencken: The iconoclast of individualism; enemy of mass conformity; critic of democracy
isabel_paterson: The God of the Machine; founding mother of American libertarianism; energy circuit of production
rose_wilder_lane: The Discovery of Freedom; individual sovereignty; founding mother of libertarianism
carl_menger: Founder of Austrian School; subjective theory of value; Principles of Economics (1871); methodological individualism
eugen_bohm_bawerk: Capital and Interest; critique of Marx exploitation theory; time preference; roundabout production
ludwig_von_mises: Human Action; praxeology; rational economic calculation; critique of socialism
henry_hazlitt: Economics in One Lesson; seen and unseen; close friend and ally of Ayn Rand
friedrich_hayek: Spontaneous order; Road to Serfdom; knowledge problem; critique of central planning
milton_friedman: Capitalism and Freedom; monetarism; empiricist methodology; free markets; school choice
george_stigler: Regulatory capture theory; economics of information; Nobel 1982; critique of government intervention
gary_becker: Human capital theory; economics of discrimination; Nobel 1992; rational choice applied to all behavior
thomas_sowell: Knowledge and Decisions; Basic Economics; critique of egalitarianism; constrained vs unconstrained visions
karl_jaspers: Existenz; transcendence; limits of reason; boundary situations
g_e_moore: Common sense realism; naturalistic fallacy in ethics
ludwig_wittgenstein: Late: language games dissolve objective meaning; meaning is use, not correspondence to reality
bernard_williams: Integrity and internal reasons dissolve objective moral standards — moral relativism with analytic credentials
john_searle: Chinese Room argument; social construction of reality; intentionality
saul_kripke: Naming and Necessity; rigid designators; modal logic; necessity a posteriori
a_j_ayer: Language Truth and Logic; strict verificationism
martin_heidegger: Being over cognition; Dasein; critique of Cartesian reason
jean_paul_sartre: Existence precedes essence — denies human nature; radical freedom has no rational grounding in reality
simone_de_beauvoir: Existentialist feminism; situation over nature; no objective human essence or sex-based identity
albert_camus: Absurdism: universe is irrational and indifferent; revolt without rational foundation
merleau_ponty: Embodied cognition dissolves subject-object distinction; no mind-independent reality accessible
paul_ricoeur: Hermeneutics; all understanding is interpretation; no unmediated access to objective meaning
walter_benjamin: Marxist aesthetics; dialectical image; history as catastrophe — no rational progress
max_horkheimer: Instrumental reason as domination; Enlightenment itself becomes myth — attacks reason at its root
herbert_marcuse: One-dimensional man; technological reason as repression
theodor_adorno: Negative dialectics; no positive knowledge possible; Enlightenment inevitably turns into barbarism
jurgen_habermas: Communicative reason is intersubjective not objective — truth by consensus, not by correspondence to reality
noam_chomsky: Innate grammar; generative rationalism; language universals
thomas_kuhn: Paradigm shifts; scientific revolutions incommensurable — no objective progress in science
barbara_branden: The Passion of Ayn Rand; early movement organizer
nathaniel_branden: Psychology of self-esteem; chief popularizer of Objectivism
hannah_arendt: Political action over individual reason; vita activa over vita contemplativa — the collective precedes the individual
louis_althusser: Scientific Marxism; individuals are effects of ideology — no autonomous rational subject
john_rawls: Veil of ignorance erases individual identity; distributive justice violates rights; egalitarianism over merit
robert_nozick: Libertarian natural rights; self-ownership; Anarchy State and Utopia — closest liberal to Objectivism
lyotard: Incredulity toward metanarratives; end of grand reason
jacques_derrida: Deconstruction; all texts endlessly defer meaning
alasdair_macintyre: Tradition-dependent rationality — no universal reason, only internal standards of practices
richard_rorty: No mirror of nature; truth is contingent and social
murray_rothbard: Anarcho-capitalism; utopian stateless society; competing defense agencies; anti-IP; subjectivist ethics; no objective law
hilary_putnam: No view from nowhere; internal realism denies mind-independent objective reality — Kant recycled
alvin_plantinga: Belief in God as properly basic — bypasses reason entirely; faith without rational justification is anti-reason
david_lewis: Modal realism; possible worlds; logical rigor
derek_parfit: Personal identity; objective reasons; reasons and persons
nick_bostrom: Simulation argument; existential risk; rational futurism
edwin_locke: The Prime Movers (2000); goal-setting theory; Objectivist psychology of achievement
allan_gotthelf: Gotthelf on Aristotle; philosophy of biology; Objectivist scholar
harry_binswanger: How We Know (2014); consciousness; ARI senior fellow
andrew_bernstein: The Capitalist Manifesto; Objectivism in history and culture
peter_schwartz: In Defense of Selfishness (2015); The Foreign Policy of Self-Interest; ARI senior fellow
yaron_brook: Equal Is Unfair; ARI executive chairman; Objectivist activism
robert_mayhew: s Q&A volumes; Essays on Ayn Rand
darryl_wright: Objectivist ethics and metaethics; Harvey Mudd College; normative theory
craig_biddle: Loving Life (2002); The Objective Standard; Objectivist ethics
gregory_salmieri: A Companion to Ayn Rand (2016); Objectivist epistemology
elan_journo: Winning the Unwinnable War (2009); ARI fellow; foreign policy and self-interest
don_watkins: Equal Is Unfair (2016); Free Market Revolution; ARI fellow; capitalism and rights
david_kelley: The Evidence of the Senses; open Objectivism; Atlas Society
robert_tracinski: The Tracinski Letter; Rand-influenced cultural and political commentary
walter_block: Rothbardian anarcho-capitalism; utopian; anti-IP; NAP without objective law; subjectivist framework
david_friedman: Consequentialist anarcho-capitalism; utopian; competing private defense agencies; utilitarian ethics; anti-IP
hans_hermann_hoppe: Kantian argumentation ethics (transcendental deduction); utopian natural order; anti-democracy and anti-objective-law; praxeology; anti-IP
peter_singer: Impartial utilitarianism; rational but demands self-sacrifice; animal rights dissolve human exceptionalism
martha_nussbaum: Capabilities require redistribution overriding individual rights; cosmopolitan obligations deny national sovereignty
sam_harris: Moral landscape; science can determine values; critique of religion and faith
christopher_hitchens: Antitheism; religion poisons everything; Enlightenment values; free expression
richard_dawkins: Selfish gene; memes; evolution as explanation; militant atheism; God delusion
slavoj_zizek: Lacanian Marxism; the Real is inaccessible; ideology pervades all apparent rationality
